title: Doctors on Strike: What Patients Need to Know
summary: In England, some doctors called resident doctors are going on strike for six days starting Tuesday. The NHS, which runs hospitals and clinics, says patients should still get the care they need. Senior doctors and other doctors not on strike will be working. Patients should go to their appointments unless told not to. The strike is about pay and job chances for these doctors. It might be hard for the NHS, but they have plans to keep services safe. If you are sick, call 111 or 999 for emergencies, or see your regular doctor for less urgent problems. The government and doctors are trying to fix the problem.

the National Health Service, which gives free healthcare in England

planned times to see a doctor

the money workers get for their job

questions.question: How long is the doctors' strike in England?
What should patients do if they have a planned appointment during the strike?
Who will be working during the strike?
What number should you call for a health emergency in England?

background_read: In England, doctors work for the NHS to help sick people. Sometimes, doctors go on strike, which means they stop working to ask for better pay or conditions. This can make it hard for patients to get care, but the NHS tries to have other doctors cover the work. Strikes are a way for workers to show they are unhappy and want changes. It's important for patients to know what to do during a strike to stay safe and healthy.
Article_Structure: WHO: Resident doctors in England and the NHS
WHAT: A six-day strike about pay and job chances, with patients told to seek care as normal
WHY: To resolve a dispute over pay and job opportunities for doctors

perspectives.description: The NHS says patients should still get care and has plans to keep services running during the strike.
Doctors are striking to ask for better pay and job chances, saying they are tired and unhappy.
